item.title: PatchGuard Internals: Initialization, Contexts and Verification
item.description:
Summary

A multi-part research series reverse engineering PatchGuard (Kernel Patch Protection) across Windows 10 and 11 builds. Covers the initialization routine, encrypted context storage, DPC-based verification scheduling, and the final KeBugCheckEx call chain triggered on integrity violation.

Parts Covered

  1. PatchGuard initialization and context allocation
  2. Encrypted context structures and obfuscation keys
  3. DPC timer scheduling and verification triggers
  4. Detection bypass techniques (historical) and Microsoft’s mitigations

Read More

See the full series: PatchGuard Analysis
